response.reasoning: Candidate has 15+ years of software engineering experience, well above the 6+ year requirement. Strong full-stack background with TypeScript, Node.js, React, and MongoDB matches the primary stack closely. Current role at re:cinq explicitly involves AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generators, directly hitting the AI agent development requirement. Prior roles at Optilyz (TypeScript/AWS/React/MongoDB) and RIDE (Node/TS/GraphQL with TDD/CI/CD) align well. Founder of GISCollective shows architecture ownership and Kubernetes deployment experience. Gaps: no explicit Nest.js, Vitest, Kotlin, or Java-for-backend-services mentioned (Java appears only in older robotics work); no PostgreSQL listed; cloud experience is AWS-focused. DLang heritage is unusual but not disqualifying. Strong testing/TDD culture, workshops, and craftsmanship orientation fit the 'quality and best practices' responsibility.
strengths: Deep full-stack TypeScript/Node.js/React experience with hands-on AI-agent and MCP work
15+ years across web, mobile, embedded; strong TDD, CI/CD, and architecture track record
Proven delivery on production systems (Optilyz pricing, RIDE, Dunnhumby high-throughput services)
concerns: No explicit Nest.js or Vitest experience listed in resume
Kotlin missing and Java only appears in older robotics/embedded context, not modern backend services
PostgreSQL not mentioned; relational DB exposure limited to MySQL/SQL generically

key_factors.factor: Senior tenure, TDD and architecture craftsmanship
Missing Nest.js, Vitest, Kotlin specifics from primary stack

response.justification: Candidate clearly exceeds the experience bar and matches the core TypeScript/Node/React/AI-agent profile, including current MCP and agentic workflow work that directly addresses the AI agent requirement. Specific framework gaps (Nest.js, Vitest, Kotlin) are learnable for an engineer of this depth and shouldn't block a conversation. An interview is warranted to confirm framework-specific fluency and relational DB depth.
